claims: intent fully realized in current working tree
code frozen and identical since prior planning gates
fallback reviewer verdict is real and counted, not proceed_degraded
sole residual is operational (pytest not executed)
confidence_criteria: all 5 intent reqs verified in current source (met)
working-tree diff matches frozen planning hashes (met)
replay failure_summary null and artifact_rigor clean (met)
outcome_review-stage recorded reviewer verdict (NOT met; produced now over identical code)
outcome.confidence_rationale: Code correctness and completeness are verified live against current source with byte-identical diff and null failure_summary, giving high confidence the intent is realized. Capped below 0.9 because the test suite was never executed (test_status unknown) and the recorded dual-agent verdict is from the tdd_review snapshot rather than an outcome_review-stage run.

assumptions_to_verify: The six listed tests pass when executed (currently inferred from source, not observed)
No environment-specific path makes the classifier diverge from inspected behavior at runtime
contradictions_checked: outcome-review.md says 'No events recorded' while gate is requested — resolved: I produced the verdict now against frozen-identical code (diff byte-matches replay manifest)

Risk access-denied classifier over-triggers and swallows transient Cursor failures — refuted: cursor_agent.py:1104-1107 requires 401/403 status or marker+code, so generic internal errors stay reviewer_infrastructure_unavailable/recoverable

Risk gate accepts via masked fallback on access-denied — refuted: fallback preserves reviewer_access_denied (cursor_agent.py:363) and stdio.py:3681 returns it as non-recoverable before the recoverable branch
missing_evidence: Live pytest pass/fail for the two focused suites and the full suite
An outcome_review-stage recorded dual-agent reviewer verdict (outcome-review.md has no events)
critical_review.strongest_objection: No observed-green test run: the entire correctness argument rests on source inspection; RED-then-GREEN is asserted but never executed because Bash/pytest approval was not granted, and the exported reviewer verdict is from the tdd_review snapshot rather than an outcome_review-stage run.
critical_review.what_would_change_my_mind: A live pytest run showing any of the six tests fail or error, or evidence the classifier misclassifies a genuine transient Cursor failure as reviewer_access_denied (which would break the transient recovery net).

decisions: accept: all 5 intent requirements realized and verified live in current source
accept: working-tree diff is byte-identical to frozen replay manifest diff_stat (525/9, 4 files)
accept: access-denied classifier is conservative (requires 401/403), excluded from recoverable infra, no retry; will not swallow genuine transient Cursor failures
accept: fallback reviewer produced a real counted independent verdict, not proceed_degraded — the core intent
objections: test_status unknown: focused and full pytest suites were not run because Bash approval was not granted (operational, not a code defect)
exported result reflects the tdd_review gate snapshot; no events were recorded at the outcome_review gate, so the dual-agent reviewer evidence is from tdd_review over frozen-identical code

specialists.name: lead-outcome-reviewer
specialists.objection: observed-green pytest not run (Bash approval not granted); correctness verified by source inspection

specialists.name: lead-tdd-reviewer
specialists.objection: pytest not executed so observed-green is unverified; non-blocking

outcome.summary: Outcome review for reviewer-route-access-fix. All 5 intent requirements verified live in current source: non-recoverable cursor_reviewer_access_denied returned before the recoverable branch (stdio.py:3681), litellm/Gemini fallback that preserves access-denied (cursor_agent.py:363), conservative 401/403 classifier (cursor_agent.py:1104-1107), recoverable=False with no retry on access-denied (cursor_agent.py:944), and access-denied excluded from the recoverable-infrastructure set (stdio.py:3794-3801). Working-tree diff (525 ins/9 del, 4 files) is byte-identical to the replay manifest diff_stat; failure_summary is null; artifact_rigor is clean and not user-facing (no screenshot block). The fallback reviewer produced a real counted ACCEPT verdict rather than proceed_degraded, which is exactly the intent. ACCEPT; sole residual is operational: observed-green pytest was not run (Bash approval not granted), so test_status is unknown.

claims: intent fully realized in current working tree
code frozen and identical since prior planning gates
fallback reviewer verdict is real and counted, not proceed_degraded
sole residual is operational (pytest not executed)
